description_long: This sculpture is a vertically oriented, intricately carved stone pillar with organic, flowing forms and abstract motifs. The central shaft is adorned with twisting, vine-like elements that wrap around and create negative spaces, giving the piece a sense of movement and complexity. The top of the sculpture features a rounded, irregular mass that appears almost cloud-like or eroded, contrasting with the more structured lower section. The base is rectangular and solid, providing stability and a clear separation from the ground. The surface is rough and matte, with visible chisel marks and natural stone texture. The coloration is a warm, light beige with subtle variations, typical of weathered stone.
description: A tall, intricately carved stone pillar sculpture with organic, twisting forms and a rough, matte surface. The piece features a solid rectangular base and a rounded, abstract top.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full detail of the twisting, vine-like carvings and the central shaft, with the abstract top clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the carvings and the irregular, organic contours along the length of the pillar.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the sculpture, with less intricate carving but maintaining the overall organic form and negative spaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the thickness of the pillar and the asymmetry of the top and base.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific environment.
